source.title: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA Guideline for the Management of Heart Failure

signals: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA Guideline for the Management of Heart Failure
American College of Cardiology/American Heart Association Joint Committee on Clinical Practice Guidelines

extraction.summary: This 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA guideline provides comprehensive evidence-based recommendations for heart failure prevention, diagnosis, and management across all stages. Key updates include expanding GDMT for HFrEF to include 4 medication classes including SGLT2i, new recommendations for HFmrEF and HFpEF populations, and updated staging terminology.

population.risk_criteria: Patients at risk for heart failure (Stage A), pre-HF (Stage B), symptomatic HF (Stage C), or advanced HF (Stage D)
population.disease_definition: Heart failure defined by stages: Stage A (at risk), Stage B (pre-HF with structural heart disease), Stage C (symptomatic HF), Stage D (advanced HF)
population.exclusions: No specific exclusions mentioned in this excerpt
population.severity_staging: HFrEF (LVEF ≤40%), HFmrEF (LVEF 41-49%), HFpEF (LVEF ≥50%), HFimpHF (improved LVEF >40% from previously reduced)

key_thresholds.context: Reduced ejection fraction heart failure classification
Mildly reduced ejection fraction heart failure
Previously HFrEF patients with improved LVEF
Echocardiographic evidence of elevated filling pressures
Strong evidence of elevated filling pressures
Screening threshold for echocardiography and cardiology referral
Threshold for ICD consideration in genetic cardiomyopathy
Dietary sodium restriction in HF patients
Minimum heart rate for ivabradine consideration
Target range to avoid increased mortality risk
Criteria for transcatheter edge-to-edge mitral valve repair
Optimal range for transcatheter edge-to-edge repair benefit

recommendations.recommendation: GDMT for HFrEF now includes 4 medication classes including SGLT2 inhibitors
recommendations.notes: Key update expanding standard therapy

recommendations.recommendation: SGLT2i for HFmrEF
recommendations.notes: Class of Recommendation 2a

recommendations.recommendation: SGLT2i for HFpEF
recommendations.notes: Class of Recommendation 2a

recommendations.recommendation: Continue HFrEF treatment in patients with improved LVEF >40%
recommendations.notes: Patients should continue therapy despite LVEF improvement

recommendations.recommendation: Treat hypertension in HFpEF
recommendations.notes: Class of Recommendation 1

recommendations.recommendation: Classify heart failure by stages A-D to emphasize disease progression and guide therapeutic interventions
recommendations.notes: Stage-based approach allows for targeted prevention and treatment strategies

recommendations.recommendation: Use LVEF to classify HF patients (HFrEF ≤40%, HFmrEF 41-49%, HFpEF ≥50%) for prognosis and treatment decisions
recommendations.notes: EF classification guides evidence-based treatment selection

recommendations.recommendation: Require evidence of elevated LV filling pressures for diagnosis of HFmrEF and HFpEF to improve diagnostic specificity
recommendations.notes: Elevated natriuretic peptides or hemodynamic evidence needed beyond EF and symptoms

recommendations.recommendation: Continue HFrEF treatment in patients with improved EF (HFimpEF) as EF may decline with treatment withdrawal
recommendations.notes: Structural abnormalities often persist despite EF improvement

recommendations.recommendation: In patients with HF, vital signs and evidence of clinical congestion should be assessed at each encounter to guide overall management
recommendations.notes: Includes adjustment of diuretics and other medications

recommendations.recommendation: In patients presenting with dyspnea, measurement of BNP or NT-proBNP is useful to support diagnosis or exclusion of HF
recommendations.notes: Higher sensitivity than specificity, more useful for ruling out HF

recommendations.recommendation: In patients with chronic HF, measurements of BNP or NT-proBNP levels are recommended for risk stratification
recommendations.notes: Associated with greater risk for adverse outcomes

recommendations.recommendation: In patients at risk of developing HF, BNP or NT-proBNP-based screening followed by team-based care can be useful to prevent development of LV dysfunction
recommendations.notes: Should include cardiovascular specialist involvement

recommendations.recommendation: In first-degree relatives of selected patients with genetic or inherited cardiomyopathies, genetic screening and counseling are recommended
recommendations.notes: To detect cardiac disease and prompt consideration of treatments to decrease HF progression and sudden death

recommendations.recommendation: In patients with suspected or new-onset HF, transthoracic echocardiography should be performed during initial evaluation
recommendations.notes: To assess cardiac structure and function

recommendations.recommendation: In patients with HF, routine use of invasive hemodynamic monitoring is not recommended
recommendations.notes: No benefit shown in routine management

recommendations.recommendation: In patients with LVEF ≤40%, ACE inhibitors should be used to prevent symptomatic HF and reduce mortality
recommendations.notes: Stage B pre-HF patients

recommendations.recommendation: In patients with recent or remote history of MI or ACS, statins should be used to prevent symptomatic HF and adverse cardiovascular events
recommendations.notes: Stage B pre-HF patients

recommendations.recommendation: In patients with hypertension, blood pressure should be controlled in accordance with GDMT to prevent symptomatic HF
recommendations.notes: Stage A primary prevention

recommendations.recommendation: In patients with type 2 diabetes and established CVD or high cardiovascular risk, SGLT2 inhibitors should be used to prevent HF hospitalizations
recommendations.notes: Stage A primary prevention

recommendations.recommendation: In selected ambulatory patients with HF, cardiopulmonary exercise testing is recommended to determine appropriateness of advanced treatments
recommendations.notes: For LVAD or heart transplant evaluation

recommendations.recommendation: In patients at least 40 days post-MI with LVEF ≤30% and NYHA class I symptoms while receiving GDMT, an ICD is recommended for primary prevention of SCD
recommendations.notes: With reasonable expectation of meaningful survival >1 year

recommendations.recommendation: ACE inhibitors should be used in patients with LVEF ≤40% to prevent symptomatic HF and reduce mortality
recommendations.notes: Class I recommendation

recommendations.recommendation: Statins should be used in patients with recent or remote history of MI or ACS to prevent symptomatic HF
recommendations.notes: Class I recommendation

recommendations.recommendation: Evidence-based beta-blockers should be used in patients with recent/remote MI or ACS and LVEF ≤40%
recommendations.notes: Class I recommendation

recommendations.recommendation: ICD recommended for primary prevention of SCD in patients ≥40 days post-MI with LVEF ≤30%
recommendations.notes: NYHA class I symptoms on GDMT with >1 year life expectancy

recommendations.recommendation: Patients with HF should receive care from multidisciplinary teams
recommendations.notes: To facilitate GDMT implementation and reduce rehospitalization

recommendations.recommendation: Thiazolidinediones should not be used in patients with LVEF <50%
recommendations.notes: Class III: Harm - increases HF risk including hospitalizations

recommendations.recommendation: Exercise training is recommended for patients with HF who are able to participate to improve functional status, exercise performance, and QOL
recommendations.notes: Safe and beneficial in stable HFrEF and HFpEF patients

recommendations.recommendation: In patients with HF who have fluid retention, diuretics are recommended to relieve congestion, improve symptoms, and prevent worsening HF
recommendations.notes: Should be combined with other GDMT, not used in isolation

recommendations.recommendation: In patients with HFrEF and NYHA class II to III symptoms, the use of ARNi is recommended to reduce morbidity and mortality
recommendations.notes: Preferred over ACEi when feasible

recommendations.recommendation: In patients with HFrEF, use of 1 of the 3 beta blockers proven to reduce mortality (bisoprolol, carvedilol, sustained-release metoprolol succinate) is recommended
recommendations.notes: Should be prescribed unless contraindicated or not tolerated

recommendations.recommendation: Use bisoprolol, carvedilol, or sustained-release metoprolol succinate to reduce mortality and hospitalizations in HFrEF
recommendations.notes: Class 1A recommendation

recommendations.recommendation: MRA (spironolactone or eplerenone) recommended for NYHA class II-IV HFrEF if eGFR >30 and K+ <5.0
recommendations.notes: Requires careful monitoring

recommendations.recommendation: SGLT2i recommended to reduce HF hospitalization and CV mortality regardless of diabetes status
recommendations.notes: For symptomatic chronic HFrEF

recommendations.recommendation: Hydralazine-isosorbide dinitrate for African American patients with NYHA III-IV on optimal therapy
recommendations.notes: Race-specific recommendation

recommendations.recommendation: Discontinue MRA if serum potassium cannot be maintained <5.5 mEq/L
recommendations.notes: Harm recommendation

recommendations.recommendation: Titrate GDMT medications to target doses achieved in clinical trials to reduce cardiovascular mortality and HF hospitalizations
recommendations.notes: Unless not well tolerated

recommendations.recommendation: Dihydropyridine calcium channel-blocking drugs are not recommended for HFrEF treatment
recommendations.notes: Class 3: No Benefit recommendation

recommendations.recommendation: Nondihydropyridine calcium channel-blocking drugs are not recommended
recommendations.notes: Class 3: Harm recommendation

recommendations.recommendation: NSAIDs should be avoided or withdrawn when possible as they worsen HF symptoms
recommendations.notes: Class 3: Harm recommendation

recommendations.recommendation: Ivabradine can be beneficial to reduce HF hospitalizations and cardiovascular death in symptomatic stable chronic HFrEF patients
recommendations.notes: For patients on maximum tolerated beta-blocker with HR ≥70 bpm

recommendations.recommendation: Digoxin may be considered to decrease HF hospitalizations in symptomatic HFrEF patients despite GDMT
recommendations.notes: No mortality benefit, use with caution

recommendations.recommendation: Vericiguat may be considered in high-risk HFrEF patients with recent worsening on GDMT
recommendations.notes: Reduces HF hospitalization and CV death by 10%

recommendations.recommendation: ICD therapy recommended for primary prevention in patients with LVEF ≤35% and NYHA II-III on GDMT
recommendations.notes: Requires >1 year life expectancy

recommendations.recommendation: CRT indicated for patients with LVEF ≤35%, sinus rhythm, LBBB with QRS ≥150ms, NYHA II-IV on GDMT
recommendations.notes: Reduces mortality, hospitalizations, improves symptoms and QOL

recommendations.recommendation: Surgical revascularization plus GDMT beneficial for selected HFrEF patients with suitable coronary anatomy
recommendations.notes: Improves symptoms, CV hospitalizations, and long-term mortality

recommendations.recommendation: Surgical revascularization plus GDMT is beneficial in selected patients with HF, reduced EF (EF≤35%), and suitable coronary anatomy to improve symptoms, cardiovascular hospitalizations, and long-term all-cause mortality
recommendations.notes: STICH trial showed 10-year benefit

recommendations.recommendation: VHD should be managed in a multidisciplinary manner in accordance with clinical practice guidelines
recommendations.notes: Prevents worsening HF and adverse outcomes

recommendations.recommendation: Optimization of GDMT is recommended before any intervention for secondary MR related to LV dysfunction
recommendations.notes: Can improve MR and obviate need for intervention

recommendations.recommendation: SGLT2i can be beneficial in decreasing HF hospitalizations and cardiovascular mortality in HFmrEF
recommendations.notes: EMPEROR-Preserved trial data

recommendations.recommendation: GDMT should be continued in patients with HFimpEF to prevent relapse of HF and LV dysfunction
recommendations.notes: 40% relapse rate within 6 months if withdrawn

recommendations.recommendation: Patients with HFpEF and hypertension should have medication titrated to attain blood pressure targets
recommendations.notes: Prevents morbidity

recommendations.recommendation: SGLT2 inhibitors recommended in patients with HFpEF to reduce HF hospitalizations and cardiovascular death
recommendations.notes: 21% reduction in primary composite endpoint, 29% reduction in HF hospitalization

recommendations.recommendation: MRA can be beneficial in selected patients with HFpEF to reduce HF hospitalizations
recommendations.notes: Careful monitoring of potassium and renal function required

recommendations.recommendation: ARB may be considered in patients with HFpEF to reduce HF hospitalizations
recommendations.notes: Borderline statistical significance in trials

recommendations.recommendation: Screening for cardiac amyloidosis with serum/urine monoclonal light chains and immunofixation electrophoresis
recommendations.notes: In patients with clinical suspicion

recommendations.recommendation: Bone scintigraphy for transthyretin cardiac amyloidosis diagnosis
recommendations.notes: In patients without evidence of monoclonal light chains

recommendations.recommendation: Tafamidis for wild-type or variant transthyretin cardiac amyloidosis with NYHA Class I-III symptoms
recommendations.notes: Reduces cardiovascular morbidity and mortality but low economic value

medications.indication: Recent MI with LVEF ≤40% if ACE inhibitor intolerant
Recent or remote MI/ACS for HF prevention
Recent/remote MI or ACS with LVEF ≤40%

medications.dose: 20-40 mg once or twice daily, max 600 mg daily
5 mg twice daily (initial), 7.5 mg twice daily (target)
medications.indication: Heart rate reduction in HFrEF with HR ≥70 bpm
Symptomatic HFrEF despite GDMT to decrease HF hospitalizations
High-risk HFrEF patients with recent HF worsening on GDMT
Wild-type or variant transthyretin cardiac amyloidosis

red_flags: Advanced heart failure requiring specialty referral
Marked HF symptoms that interfere with daily life despite optimal GDMT
Signs of advanced heart failure (Stage D)
Advanced HF clinical factors identified through history and physical examination
Evidence of specific causes requiring disease-specific therapy (amyloid heart disease, sarcoidosis, hemochromatosis)
Concurrent illness (ongoing myocardial ischemia, pulmonary emboli, systemic infection)
Nonadherence to medication or dietary regimen
Rapidly progressive clinical HF or worsening ventricular dysfunction despite appropriate medical treatment
Pathogenic variants associated with increased risk of sudden death
Desmosomal cardiac disease requiring exercise restriction
Acute cardiac rejection after heart transplantation
Persistent or worsening symptoms with uncertain hemodynamics
Worsening ventricular dysfunction despite appropriate medical treatment
Suspected acute cardiac rejection after heart transplant
NYHA Class IV symptoms with inability to carry out physical activity
Patients with LVEF <50% on thiazolidinediones - increases HF hospitalization risk
Use of nondihydropyridine calcium channel blockers in patients with LVEF <50% - negative inotropic effects may be harmful
Depression, social isolation, frailty, and low health literacy as risk factors for poor self-care
History of angioedema with ACEi or ARNi
Significant renal impairment or perfusion issues
Clinical deterioration with abrupt beta-blocker withdrawal
Edema with sodium zirconium cyclosilicate use
Worsening HF symptoms with thiazolidinediones or DPP-4 inhibitors
Proarrhythmic effects with class IC antiarrhythmics
Digoxin serum concentration ≥1.2 ng/mL (increased mortality risk)
NT-proBNP >5314 pg/mL may indicate lack of vericiguat benefit
Persistent severe secondary MR despite GDMT
Persistent arrhythmias from well-defined aneurysm or scar
LV wall thickness ≥14mm with fatigue, dyspnea, or edema
Discordance between wall thickness on echo and QRS voltage on ECG
Autonomic or sensory polyneuropathy with HFpEF
Worsening renal function with MRA therapy
Critical cardiac dysfunction requiring escalating inotropic support
extraction.follow_up: Serial evaluation recommended with ongoing assessment of GDMT optimization, device therapy consideration, and monitoring for disease progression across heart failure stages
extraction.escalation_path: Patients with advanced HF who wish to prolong survival should be referred to a team specializing in HF for review of HF management, assessment of suitability for advanced HF therapies, and palliative care coordination
